privileged_info_summary: Completed PI audit for chrome_env_all web_accessibility_audit_workspace task. Confirmed that the browser profile and 22 bookmarks are synthetically generated via setup_task.sh. All metadata claims exactly match the task description and verifier logic. However, discovered two critical bugs in verifier.py's categorization scoring logic: First, the domain mapping dictionary uses the key 'Standards' instead of the requested 'Standards & Guidelines', meaning the verifier silently skips checking that folder entirely. Second, the verifier lowercases all bookmark URLs before checking for expected substrings, but contains uppercase characters in two of its expected substrings ('w3.org/WAI/standards' and 'w3.org/WAI/ARIA'), making it impossible for those specific domains to ever match. These bugs prevent an agent from achieving a perfect categorization score even with a flawless execution.
